allGaps.description: Claude claims Cursor supports 'multi-model support' including GPT-5, Opus, Gemini, xAI models. The website only mentions 'GPT-5.2 Codex Extra High Fast' and 'Agent Opus 4.6' in specific feature contexts, but never lists a general multi-model selection feature or model switcher.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Clarification on whether Cursor allows users to choose between different LLM models or if specific features are tied to specific models

evidence.claudeSays: Integrates models from OpenAI (GPT-5), Anthropic (Opus), Gemini, xAI, and proprietary Cursor models
evidence.siteContent: The website shows 'GPT-5.2 Codex Extra High Fast' and 'Agent Opus 4.6' mentioned in specific feature demonstrations, but does not describe a user-facing model selection feature or claim support for Gemini or xAI models

allGaps.description: Claude describes an 'autonomy slider' feature that allows users to control agent independence. This specific term and feature are not mentioned anywhere on the website content provided.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Clarification on whether there is an actual 'autonomy slider' UI control or if Claude inferred this from agent capability descriptions

evidence.claudeSays: Autonomy slider approach: Users control how much independence to give the AI, from simple autocomplete to fully autonomous agents
evidence.siteContent: Website describes agents that 'work autonomously' and tab autocomplete features, but does not mention an 'autonomy slider' or explicit control mechanism by that name

product.name: Cursor
product.description: An AI-powered code editor and development environment designed to accelerate software development through autonomous agents and context-aware AI assistance

positioning.forWho: Professional developers and engineering teams building production software

capabilities: Desktop IDE with AI-powered coding assistance
CLI interface for terminal-based AI help
Autonomous agents that build, test, and demo features end-to-end
Composer interface for task planning and delegation

context.stage: Active product with recent roadmap items (2026 features like Secure codebase indexing, Semantic search)

headline.description: Claude claims Cursor supports 'multi-model support' including GPT-5, Opus, Gemini, xAI models. The website only mentions 'GPT-5.2 Codex Extra High Fast' and 'Agent Opus 4.6' in specific feature contexts, but never lists a general multi-model selection feature or model switcher.
